How it works The 3D process For partners Our work About Tell us about your business

See what the 3D Process actually produces.

90 structured output types across Discover, Design and Deploy. Every output has a job. Every phase has a checkpoint.

Tell us about your business

Not a workshop. Not a prompt stack. An operating library.

Most marketing engagements produce a deck and a promise. The 3D Process produces a structured library of working documents, plans, briefs and assets, built in a fixed order so each output feeds the next. This library is what the agency-in-a-box holds.

The diagnosis in Discover becomes the strategy in Design. The strategy becomes briefs. The briefs become launched work in Deploy. And what the launched work teaches us becomes the sharper next cycle. Nothing is vague because everything has a name, a place and a review gate.

Diagnosis

Discover
19 output types · closes at CP1

We diagnose the market, customer, offer and current constraints so the strategy is grounded in evidence.

Audience and market 5
  • Audience Teardown
  • Customer Segments
  • Customer Pain Points
  • Buying Triggers and Barriers
  • Jobs To Be Done
Category and competitors 4
  • Competitor Analysis
  • Category Positioning
  • Market Gaps
  • Differentiation Opportunities
Offer and commercial diagnosis 5
  • Offer Worksheet
  • Product and Service Review
  • Pricing and Packaging Notes
  • Sales Process Review
  • Funnel Review
Discovery output 5
  • Success Definition
  • Objectives and Key Results
  • Discovery Summary
  • Priority Problems To Solve
  • Recommended Next Moves
CP1
Discovery Summary.

You review the diagnosis, the priority problems and the recommended moves. Nothing proceeds until you approve it.

Strategy

Design
40 output types · closes at CP2

We turn the discovery into strategy, messaging, brand direction, plans and briefs that can be built from.

Business and growth strategy 4
  • Business Plan
  • Strategic Priorities
  • Growth Roadmap
  • Measurement Plan
Customer and journey strategy 4
  • Customer Profile
  • Customer Journey
  • Messaging by Stage
  • Conversion Pathway
Brand and messaging 6
  • Brand Strategy
  • Positioning
  • Messaging and Offer Architecture
  • Brand Guidelines
  • Copy Deck
  • Logo Brief
Website and digital strategy 5
  • Website Strategy
  • Website Brief
  • Sitemap
  • Page Strategy
  • SEO Strategy
Sales and marketing plan 12

One parent plan, with sub-plans below.

  • Marketing Plan
  • Social Strategy
  • Content Strategy
  • In-Market Activation Plan
  • Paid Media Plan
  • EDM Plan
  • CRM Plan
  • PR Plan
  • Events Plan
  • Partnership and Referral Plan
  • Traditional Media Plan
  • Campaign Calendar
Briefing documents 9
  • Design Brief
  • Website Brief
  • Photo and Video Brief
  • SEO Brief
  • Paid Media Brief
  • EDM Brief
  • CRM Brief
  • PR Brief
  • Events Brief
CP2
Strategy Summary.

You review the strategy, the messaging and every brief before anything is produced. Approved direction becomes the build spec.

Delivery

Deploy
31 output types · closes at CP3

We launch the approved work, manage the activity, report on performance and improve what is working.

Production and delivery 9
  • Website Build and Updates
  • Landing Pages
  • Creative Assets
  • Social Assets
  • Video Assets
  • Email Templates
  • Sales Collateral
  • Forms and Automations
  • Tracking Setup
In-market activation 9
  • Campaign Launch
  • Social Content Rollout
  • Paid Media Launch
  • EDM Deployment
  • CRM Workflow Deployment
  • PR Outreach
  • Events Activation
  • Partnership Outreach
  • Traditional Media Rollout
Reporting and optimisation 8
  • Reporting Dashboard
  • Monthly Report
  • Campaign Performance Review
  • Lead Quality Review
  • SEO Performance Review
  • Paid Media Performance Review
  • CRM and EDM Performance Review
  • Recommendations and Next Actions
Deploy output 5
  • Live Asset Links
  • Reporting Pack
  • Optimisation Backlog
  • Next Sprint Priorities
  • Case Study and Evidence Pack
CP3
IMC Summary.

You approve the integrated launch plan. From here the work goes live, gets measured and gets better.

Checkpoints are where you stay in control.

CP1 closes Discover: you approve the diagnosis. CP2 closes Design: you approve the strategy and every brief before production. CP3 closes the launch plan: you approve what goes live.

At each gate a named partner reviews the work and puts their name on it before you ever see it. You review finished work, not fragments, and nothing proceeds without your approval.

Outputs become intelligence.

The library is not paperwork. It is the system's memory. Your audience teardown informs your copy deck. Your copy deck informs your website. Your reporting informs all three, next cycle.

Once the system is live, Deploy feeds back into Discover. Performance, client intelligence and proof sharpen the next cycle, so the strategy, website, content, campaigns and CRM keep getting better instead of going stale.

Deploy is where proof gets made.

The Deploy phase does not end at launch. It manages the activity, reports on performance and produces the evidence pack: live asset links, the reporting pack, the optimisation backlog and the next sprint's priorities.

That evidence is what makes the next cycle better, and it is what makes the results yours to keep. If we ever part ways, you walk away with the entire library.

Your business, through the same engine.

Tell us about your business and the first outputs you will see are your own: a real diagnosis, at CP1, before you commit further.

Tell us about your business

The agency-in-a-box. The proven method, run by a human, at AI speed. Discover, Design, Deploy, then the system learns and the next cycle gets sharper.
